name: Cova de ca na Picarola
starting_point: Km 3 of the MA-2020, at the municipal boundary of Bunyola and Santa Maria del Camí.
distance: Approximately 500 m from the parking area
equipment: 25 m rope if descending the pit in the Entrance Chamber.
description: A predominantly horizontal cave in Bunyola with multiple interconnected chambers and galleries, explorable with or without vertical equipment depending on the route. Highlights include the Speleothem Chamber with the richest formations, steep ramps including the dangerous Inferno Ramp with rockfall risk, and the Gours Chamber with delicate formations. Remains of at least one Myotragus (extinct Balearic goat-antelope) were found in the Lower Gallery.
full_description:
Description

Sala de s'Entrada (Entrance Chamber)

With dimensions of 14 x 25 x 3.5 m, with a NW-SE orientation, this is a collapse chamber where reconstructive processes are not abundant. There are two points in the chamber that allow communication with the rest of the cave. The first of them (point 2 on the survey) allows us to continue the visit using progression equipment, while point 3 on the survey avoids having to use any type of equipment to explore the cave.

Es Pou (The Well)

From point 2 on the survey, the rope head is installed on a natural anchor to descend a first drop of 10 m and a subsequent one of 4 m, which places us in the southern zone of the Galeria Llarga (Long Gallery).

Sa Saleta (The Small Chamber)

Of reduced dimensions, 6 x 8 x 2 m, it is situated partly beneath the Entrance Chamber. It has an entrance from the exterior, after overcoming two levels formed by dry stone walls. In the part farthest from the entrance, a small crawlway allows access to the Entrance Chamber.

Els Escarpes (The Escarpments)

This is a transit zone between the Pas de Pedro (Pedro's Pass), point 3 on the survey, and the Sala dets Espeleotemes (Speleothem Chamber), point 8 on the survey. An escarpment connecting with the Long Gallery runs along the east side.

Galeria Llarga (Long Gallery)

It occupies the entire east zone of the cavity and can be accessed either via the Well with progression equipment or from the Speleothem Chamber without it. The accumulation of detritus currently separates it from the Lower Gallery.

Galeria Inferior (Lower Gallery)

Measuring 2.5 x 22 x 2 m, it accumulates large rock blocks, among which remains of at least one Myotragus were found. At its center it contains an inaccessible sink pit of 3.5 m. Its ceiling is formed by rocks that, due to their size, became wedged together, forming the floor of the Long Gallery.

Sala dets Espeleotemes (Speleothem Chamber)

This zone of the cavity, measuring 9 x 20 x 2 m, is the one that harbors the most formations as well as extensional fractures with horizontal displacement. It is accessed from the Entrance Chamber from points 2 and 3 on the survey. If we want to avoid using progression equipment, we must access from point 3 on the survey. The visit to the cave can be continued either to the NW, accessing Sa Rampa (The Ramp), or to the SE, via a ramp that will bring us closer to point 6 on the survey.

Rampa de l'Ensurt (Fright Ramp)

After overcoming some tight spots, one reaches the lower zone of Sa Rampa, 16 x 28 x 2 m at the highest zone. The slope exceeds a 40% incline; despite this, we can move without having to use progression equipment. The left zone in the order of progression is the most decorated with concretions.

Rampa de l'Infern (Hell Ramp)

A dangerous place, this ramp has a considerable drop. Once the deepest zone of the ramp is reached, a fracture prevents further progress without using progression equipment. It is better to retrace our steps, as stones fall from the upper zone at this location, threatening imminent collapse. These are the dimensions of the ramp: 3.5 x 6 m.

Saleta des Gours (Gours Chamber)

With its 12 x 7.5 x 1 m, this is one of the places in the cavity where formations are most abundant, along with some gours that give it its name. Movement through this spot is best avoided, as irreparable damage can be caused to the formations.

Es Pedregal (The Rocky Ground)

This small corner of 4 x 7 m is formed by a ramp covered with stones from an upper level.
